title: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions

input_row.title: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
input_row.description: AWS CloudTrail is a web service that records AWS API calls for your account and delivers log files to you. The recorded information includes the identity of the API caller, the time of the API call, the source IP address of the API caller, the request parameters, and the response elements returned by the AWS service. CloudTrail provides a history of AWS API calls for an account, including API calls made via the Management Console, SDKs, command line tools, and higher-level AWS services (such as CloudFormation).
input_row.rationale: The AWS API call history produced by CloudTrail enables security analysis, resource change tracking, and compliance auditing. Additionally, • ensuring that a multi-region trail exists will help detect unexpected activity occurring in otherwise unused regions • ensuring that a multi-region trail exists will ensure that Global Service Logging is enabled for a trail by default to capture recordings of events generated on AWS global services • for a multi-region trail, ensuring that management events are configured for all types of Read/Writes ensures the recording of management operations that are performed on all resources in an AWS account
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if CloudTrail is enabled for all regions: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudTrail console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail 2. Click on Trails in the left navigation pane • You will be presented with a list of trails across all regions 3. Ensure that at least one Trail has Yes specified in the Multi-region trail column 4. Click on a trail via the link in the Name column 5. Ensure Logging is set to ON 6. Ensure Multi-region trail is set to Yes 7. In the section Management Events, ensure that API activity set to ALL From Command Line: 1. List all trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails 2. Ensure IsMultiRegionTrail is set to true: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> 3. Ensure IsLogging is set to true: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> 4. Ensure there is at least one fieldSelector for a trail that equals Management: • This should NOT output any results for Field: "readOnly". If either true or false is returned, one of the checkboxes (read or write) is not selected. Example of correct output: "TrailARN": "<your_trail_ARN>", "AdvancedEventSelectors": [ { "Name": "Management events selector", "FieldSelectors": [ { "Field": "eventCategory", "Equals": [ "Management" ]
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to enable global (Multi-region) CloudTrail logging: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail. 2. Click on Trails in the left navigation pane. 3. Click Get Started Now if it is presented, then: • Click Add new trail. • Enter a trail name in the Trail name box. o A trail created in the console is a multi-region trail by default. • Specify an S3 bucket name in the S3 bucket box. • Specify the AWS KMS alias under the Log file SSE-KMS encryption section, or create a new key. • Click Next. 4. Ensure the Management events check box is selected. 5. Ensure both Read and Write are checked under API activity. 6. Click Next. 7. Review your trail settings and click Create trail. From Command Line: Create a multi-region trail: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name <trail-name> --bucket-name <s3-bucket-for-cloudtrail> --is-multi-region-trail Enable multi-region on an existing trail: aws cloudtrail update-trail --name <trail-name> --is-multi-region-trail Note: Creating a CloudTrail trail via the CLI without providing any overriding options configures all read and write Management Events to be logged by default.
input_row.impact: S3 lifecycle features can be used to manage the accumulation and management of logs over time. See the following AWS resource for more information on these features: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/object-lifecycle-mgmt.html
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail is not enabled in any region.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_trail_multi_region_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the AWS CLI or boto3 SDK to verify that CloudTrail is enabled in all regions and properly configured.
2. Perform the following checks:
   - List all trails: 
     - CLI: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
     - Validate that there is at least one trail where `IsMultiRegionTrail` is `true`.
   - For each trail found, check:
     - Ensure logging is enabled:
       - CLI: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
       - Check that `IsLogging` is `true`.
     - Verify that Management Events are selected:
       - CLI: `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
       - Ensure there is at least one `AdvancedEventSelectors` entry that has `FieldSelectors` with `Field` equal to `eventCategory` and `Equals` containing `Management`.
       - Validate that the output does not include a `readOnly` field, confirming that both read and write events are being logged.
3. Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - If `IsMultiRegionTrail` is `true`, `IsLogging` is `true`, and Management Events are set to log all API activities (both read and write), then the control passes.
   - If any of these checks fails, the control is marked as non-compliant.

This automated approach allows for efficient assessment of the CloudTrail configuration across all AWS regions, ensuring compliance with the CIS benchmark for cloud security posture management.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The checks described can be effectively performed using AWS APIs, boto3, or the AWS CLI.
- AWS SDK capabilities and CLI commands are well-suited for this task, allowing for programmatic verification of CloudTrail settings across all regions.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The CLI commands provided are valid for checking CloudTrail configurations.
- The use of `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` and `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status` is appropriate for verifying trail existence and logging status.
- The check for Management Events using `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors` is valid, but the description of the `AdvancedEventSelectors` and `FieldSelectors` could be more precise. Specifically, the mention of `AdvancedEventSelectors` might be misleading as it is an optional feature; the standard `EventSelectors` should be checked for `IncludeManagementEvents`.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_trail_multi_region_logging_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific to include management events logging, such as `aws_cloudtrail_trail_multi_region_management_logging_enabled`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The check for `AdvancedEventSelectors` should be clarified. While `AdvancedEventSelectors` provide more granular control, the basic requirement is to ensure `IncludeManagementEvents` is set to `true` in `EventSelectors`.
- Consider adding a check for whether the trail is sending logs to an S3 bucket with appropriate permissions, as this is a common compliance requirement.
- The program name could be improved to better reflect the full scope of the check. Suggested name: `aws_cloudtrail_trail_multi_region_management_events_logging_enabled`.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for the encryption status of the CloudTrail logs, ensuring they are encrypted at rest.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and utilizes AWS capabilities effectively. The technical details are mostly accurate, with minor improvements suggested for clarity and completeness.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Adjust the description of the event selectors to ensure clarity between `EventSelectors` and `AdvancedEventSelectors`. Update the program name for better specificity regarding management events logging. Consider adding checks for S3 bucket permissions and log encryption.
- The audit approach remains Automated, as it is appropriate for the described checks.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach can be automated using AWS CLI or boto3 SDK, which allows for programmatic verification of CloudTrail settings across all regions. The necessary properties, such as `IsMultiRegionTrail`, `IsLogging`, and `IncludeManagementEvents`, are accessible through AWS APIs, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_trail_multi_region_management_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: CloudTrail
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
- Properties to Check:
  - `IsMultiRegionTrail` should be `true`
  - `IsLogging` should be `true`
  - `IncludeManagementEvents` should be `true` in `EventSelectors`
- Pass Criteria:
  - At least one trail has `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `true`
  - `IsLogging` is `true` for the trail
  - `IncludeManagementEvents` is `true` in `EventSelectors`
- Fail Criteria:
  - No trail with `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `true`
  - `IsLogging` is `false`
  - `IncludeManagementEvents` is not set to `true`

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation, and the second opinion provided a slight improvement suggestion regarding the program name, which has been incorporated.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v6.0.0
